entities.name: tirzepatide

attributes.fatty_acid_modification: C20 fatty diacid moiety at position 20 lysine epsilon-amino group

entities.context: Tirzepatide is a dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist peptide with CAS Registry Number 2023788-19-2.

entities.name: GIP receptor
entities.context: The dual agonist mechanism activates both the GIP receptor (primary) and the GLP-1 receptor, with an imbalanced potency favoring GIP receptor activation.

entities.name: GLP-1 receptor
entities.context: The dual agonist mechanism activates both the GIP receptor (primary) and the GLP-1 receptor, with an imbalanced potency favoring GIP receptor activation.

entities.name: dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonism
entities.context: The dual agonist mechanism activates both the GIP receptor (primary) and the GLP-1 receptor, with an imbalanced potency favoring GIP receptor activation.

entities.name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
entities.context: Tirzepatide has demonstrated superior efficacy in glycemic control (HbA1c reduction) and weight loss compared to selective GLP-1 receptor agonists in the SURPASS clinical trial program.

entities.name: Obesity
entities.context: Tirzepatide has demonstrated superior efficacy in glycemic control (HbA1c reduction) and weight loss compared to selective GLP-1 receptor agonists in the SURPASS clinical trial program.

entities.name: SURPASS clinical trial program
entities.context: Tirzepatide has demonstrated superior efficacy in glycemic control (HbA1c reduction) and weight loss compared to selective GLP-1 receptor agonists in the SURPASS clinical trial program.

entities.name: HbA1c reduction

relations.evidence: Tirzepatide is a dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist peptide with CAS Registry Number 2023788-19-2.
The dual agonist mechanism activates both the GIP receptor (primary) and the GLP-1 receptor, with an imbalanced potency favoring GIP receptor activation.
The dual agonist mechanism activates both the GIP receptor (primary) and the GLP-1 receptor, with an imbalanced potency favoring GIP receptor activation.
Tirzepatide has demonstrated superior efficacy in glycemic control (HbA1c reduction) and weight loss compared to selective GLP-1 receptor agonists in the SURPASS clinical trial program.
